title: Cheap-Stop Slowdown Score
summary: Safety Car/VSC periods reduce pit-loss delta and cause opportunistic pit stops.
rationale: Safety Car/VSC periods reduce pit-loss delta and cause opportunistic pit stops. The tried race-lap average pace feature is too blunt; a robust field-slowdown score with rolling baselines should better detect cheap-stop windows.
implementation_hint: For each Race, compute rolling prior median/IQR of field lap times by LapNumber; create lap_slowdown_z, field_compression proxies, fraction of drivers with extreme slow laps, current PitStop share, and interactions with TyreLife and RaceProgress. Use only prior rolling baselines, then attach current-lap slowdown signals available in test.
expected_effect: Could boost recall for clustered next-lap pits after neutralizations or weather-like field slowdowns.
risk: If synthetic data lacks true SC/VSC dynamics, this may add redundant noise; current PitStop share may dominate and overfit validation if split randomly.
